forecasts.reasoning: The deadline for an official announcement is July 1, 2025, which is less than 8 weeks from the May 7, 2025 knowledge cutoff. On May 5, Premier Danielle Smith explicitly established that an independence referendum would only occur if a citizen-led petition succeeds. Under the newly introduced Bill 54, this process requires gathering approximately 177,000 signatures within a 120-day window. Even if organizers launch the petition immediately, completing the signature drive and having Elections Alberta verify them will take months, making it practically impossible to fulfill these requirements and officially schedule a referendum before July 1. Furthermore, bypassing this process to unilaterally schedule a vote would contradict Smith's stated strategy of managing separatist pressure via citizen initiative. Given the restrictive timeframe and logistical realities of the petition mechanism, an official scheduling announcement before July 1 is highly unlikely.

On April 14, 2025, Starlink was granted a 10-year official license to operate in Lesotho. Furthermore, the company was permitted to operate as a 100% foreign-owned entity, bypassing local equity requirements which often slow down implementation (as has been the case in other African markets where local joint-ventures or partners were mandated). The critical hurdle — regulatory approval — has already been definitively cleared. Looking at similar situations across Africa, the time from license approval to official ‘Availability’ on the Starlink map is typically between 1 and 3 months. The delay to July 1 represents roughly 2.5 months (78 days from license approval). Given Starlink's expanding satellite coverage and operations in neighboring Eswatini, Botswana, Zimbabwe, and Mozambique, ground infrastructure and regional logistics are already mature. Therefore, there is a high likelihood the service will be turned on and listed as ‘Available’ on the official map prior to July 1, 2025.

The resolution window is exactly 33-34 days (May 13 to June 14). Both sides appear to be in a prolonged diplomatic standoff following retaliatory tariffs in February and the cancellation of a previously scheduled call. As of mid-April, there were "no existing plans" for a call, and Trump noted he is in "no rush" to speak. Xi is also likely averse to initiating contact in a way that suggests he is caving to tariff pressure. Historically, US-China presidential phone calls occur perhaps 1-3 times a year. The base rate for a call in any random one-month period is around 10-25%, but given the current freeze and lack of an apparent catalyst scheduled for this specific mid-May to mid-June timeframe, the probability is lower than average. I estimate a 12% chance.

Massive protests occurred in Turkey in March 2025 following the arrest of Istanbul Mayor Ekrem İmamoğlu, drawing hundreds of thousands of participants. The Carnegie Global Protest Tracker has already noted these protests in its April 2025 update. Given the scale of the protests heavily eclipses the 10,000 participant threshold and they are already acknowledged by the resolving source (the Tracker), a YES resolution is nearly guaranteed before July 1, 2025.
